rationale.root_cause: In CWE415_Double_Free__malloc_free_char_01_bad, data is freed once and then freed again (around lines 32–34), causing a double free on the same pointer. The second free operates on an already-freed pointer, triggering undefined behavior.
rationale.local_fix: Delete the second free call or set data = NULL immediately after the first free so a subsequent free is a no-op. This ensures the pointer is only freed once in this function.
rationale.global_fix: Use a single-owner cleanup pattern so each allocation has exactly one responsible free site (e.g., RAII/unique owner or a centralized free helper that nulls the pointer). This enforces exactly-one-free across the codebase and prevents double frees of the same allocation.
